title: marcgravia polyantha subsp. occidentalis
intro: Marcgravia polyantha subsp. occidentalis, also known as the 'Wild Hog Plum', is a shrub or small tree native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Marcgravia polyantha subsp. occidentalis include treating various ailments, such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-diarrheal properties. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve using its leaves and bark in teas, infusions, and decoctions, although further research is needed to standardize and validate its uses.
